india_trends.title: Strategy didn’t purchase bitcoin last week
US stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 futures slip as renewed Middle East tensions lift oil prices
india_trends.summary: Investor anxiety surged as US stock futures fluctuated amid reports of a US warship incident near the Strait of Hormuz. While concerns over oil supply disruptions grow, April's robust corporate earnings helped maintain market momentum despite geopolitical risks.

india_trends.title: Carlyle buys majority stake in Knack, EqualizeRCM to build AI-led healthcare billing platform
india_trends.summary: The private equity firm is betting on consolidation in a fragmented US billing services market, with plans to scale via acquisitions and global delivery

india_trends.title: Quote of the day by Walter Schloss: "One of the things you learn in this business is humility because you see your mistakes the next day."
india_trends.summary: Walter Schloss highlights that investing demands humility, as markets quickly expose mistakes through immediate feedback. Rather than avoiding errors, successful investors learn from them—using humility to adapt, cut losses, and refine strategies.

india_trends.title: Bajaj Auto and 4 other stocks hit a 52-week high, rally up to 40% in a month
india_trends.summary: Five BSE 100 stocks, including Adani Power, Adani Ports, Nestle India, Cummins India and Bajaj Auto, hit fresh 52-week highs after rallying up to 40% over the past month.

Why the RBI is weighing a rare 2013-crisis playbook to arrest rupee slide to record lows?
india_trends.summary: The rupee’s slide to fresh record lows and oil above $100 has prompted the RBI to revisit crisis-era tools from 2013, including a special NRI dollar deposit window and possible tax relief for foreign bond investors. With reserves dipping and outflows rising, the central bank is exploring ways to att

india_trends.title: Sebi proposes changes to SDI rules to facilitate growth in listed securitisation market
india_trends.summary: Markets regulator Sebi on Monday proposed a series of changes to norms governing securitised debt instruments (SDIs), including allowing single-asset securitisation by RBI-regulated entities, winding up of securitisation transactions and easing certain structural restrictions to boost market develop

india_trends.title: NSE launches Electronic Gold Receipts to formalise India’s gold market; successfully dematerialises 1 kg gold bar
india_trends.summary: The National Stock Exchange introduced Electronic Gold Receipts, enabling dematerialised gold trading backed by physical assets in regulated vaults. The initiative enhances transparency, liquidity, and accessibility, bridging physical gold with financial markets for investors, jewellers, and institu

india_trends.title: Central Bank of India expects limited impact from new provisioning rules
india_trends.summary: Central Bank of India managing director and CEO Kalyan Kumar says it is well buffered against the RBI’s shift to stricter, forward-looking loan-loss provisioning, with existing provisions and strong capital expected to absorb any transition impact,

india_trends.title: Supervision must look beyond formal compliance, says RBI Deputy Guv Swaminathan J
india_trends.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Swaminathan J. warns that weak oversight in banking can lead to hidden risks. He stresses that supervision must go beyond reported numbers to understand actual business operations. This approach is crucial for protecting depositors and the wider economy. Digital banking brings ne

india_trends.title: Fertiliser subsidy bill set to overshoot budget estimate of Rs 1.71 lakh cr amid West Asia crisis
india_trends.summary: India's fertiliser subsidy bill for fiscal 2026-27 is set to surpass its budget. Rising costs of imported urea and other fertilisers are driving this increase. Despite global supply chain issues, fertiliser availability for the kharif 2026 season remains strong. Domestic production and imports are b

india_trends.title: Earnings Boom: 10 stocks with over 100% profit growth in Q4FY26
india_trends.summary: ​Several Nifty 500 companies reported over 100% year-on-year net profit growth in Q4FY26, reflecting strong earnings momentum. Firms like Eternal, India Cements, Kajaria Ceramics, and Chennai Petroleum led gains, showcasing improved profitability across sectors including finance, banking, and manufa

india_trends.title: NCLAT sets aside NCLT insolvency order against Embassy Developments
india_trends.summary: Embassy Developments has won a significant legal battle. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has cancelled an earlier order that placed the company into insolvency proceedings. This ruling brings an end to the corporate insolvency resolution process. The developer's operations and projects r

india_trends.title: Manappuram Finance Q4 Results: Firm swings to black with ₹405 crore profit, announces ₹0.50 dividend
india_trends.summary: Manappuram Finance's Q4 consolidated profit came in at ₹404.79 crore as against a loss of ₹203.17 crore in the same period a year ago. Meanwhile, on a quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) basis, the figure rose 69% from ₹238.55 crore in the December quarter.

india_trends.title: Gold, silver rates today nosedive up to 5% as crude oil price soars after escalation in the US-Iran war
india_trends.summary: US-Iran war: According to the News Agency Reuters, Iran hit two missiles at a US warship on Monday, when it was sailing near Jask island

india_trends.title: Crude oil jumps over 5% as US-Iran tensions flare again; Brent crude climbs past $114
india_trends.summary: Crude oil prices rose on May 4 after a tanker was reportedly hit in the Strait of Hormuz. Tensions escalated between the U.S. and Iran, with Trump promising to guide ships through the strait, while Iran warned of military action against US forces and urged coordination for passing vessels.

india_trends.title: India must map, monitor economic vulnerabilities in energy, food, other areas: EAC-PM chairman
india_trends.summary: Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) Chairman S Mahendra Dev in an interview with PTI further said the ongoing West Asia conflict highlights the need for a forward-looking approach to risk management.

india_trends.title: Rupee falls 39 paise to end at all-time low of 95.23 against US dollar — What's plaguing the domestic currency?
india_trends.summary: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at 94.95 against the US dollar, then lost ground and finally settled for the day at 95.23 (provisional), registering a fall of 39 paise over its previous close.
